name: Sunol Water Temple
summary_en: Archive documents describe the Sunol Water Temple as a historic property on the SFWD/PUC watershed, including a 2005 history noting the Spring Valley Water Company built it in 1910, a 2006 environmental impact report, and a 1997 Americans with Disabilities Act transition plan noting the temple was closed to the public. Later Arts Commission agendas and Public Utilities Commission minutes track a rehabilitation and restoration project, and 1999 minutes reference Water Department Contract No. WD-2290 for Phase 1 building restoration. The monument is operated by the Public Utilities Commission, whose FY2025 budget line is $1.8B; no place-specific city contract appears in this data.
